identificationModule.briefTitle: Fractional Flow Reserve-guided Stenting Versus Medical Therapy in Atherosclerosis Renal Artery Stenosis
identificationModule.officialTitle: Fractional Flow Reserve-guided Percutaneous Renal Artery Stenting Plus Optimal Medical Therapy Versus Optimal Medical Therapy Alone In Atherosclerosis Renal-vascular Hypertension Patients: a Multicenter Randomized Trial

descriptionModule.briefSummary:
Although randomized trials have demonstrated there is no benefit of renal-artery stenting in addition to medical therapy for patients with atherosclerosis renal artery stenosis, many patients indeed gained benefit in daily practices after stenting, such as reduction in blood pressure and recovery in renal functions. One important gap is that there is no universal standard to determine whether to stent in these patients. Fraction Flow Reserve (FFR) has been studied for many year in chronic coronary heart disease and FFR-guided revascularization strategy is known to be better than both angiography-guided revascularization and medication alone. Based on the primary finding of FAIR-pilot study (NCT05732077), FFR-guided renal artery stenting is practical.

The overall purpose of the FAIR trial is to compare the clinical outcomes and safety of FFR-guided stenting plus optimal medical treatment (OMT) versus OMT alone in patients with renal-vascular hypertensive patients.

With the 'all comers' design, participants met the inclusive/exclusive criteria will be enrolled, and hyperemic FFR induced by dopamine will be measured in all participants. If FFR is ≥0.80, patients will be treated with OMT alone and follow up. If FFR is \<0.80, participants will be randomized to stenting in the renal artery plus OMT or OMT alone on a 1:1 ratio. The blood pressure and anti-hypertensive medications will be compared before and 3 months after the procedure based on ambulatory blood pressure monitoring, all participants will be followed up for 1 year.

armGroups.label: Stenting plus OMT with FFR <0.80
interventionNames: Diagnostic Test: Fractional Flow Reserve, Renal

armGroups.label: OMT alone with FFR < 0.80
interventionNames: Diagnostic Test: Fractional Flow Reserve, Renal

armGroups.label: OMT alone with FFR ≥0.80
interventionNames: Diagnostic Test: Fractional Flow Reserve, Renal

interventions.name: Dopamine
interventions.description: A bolus dose of 50μg/kg dopamine via renal artery to induce hyperemic status

interventions.name: Fractional Flow Reserve, Renal
interventions.description: Renal FFR will be measured based on SOP

interventions.name: Renal artery stenting
interventions.description: Renal artery stenting will be implanted based on the protocol

primaryOutcomes.measure: Change in daytime mean systolic blood pressure as measured by 24-hour Ambulatory Blood Pressure Monitoring (ABPM)
Change in the composite index of antihypertensive drugs
primaryOutcomes.description: Change in the composite index of antihypertensive drugs. Drug Composite Index = Weight (number of classes of antihypertensive drugs) × (sum of doses)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in systolic blood pressure as measured by 24-hour ABPM
Change in diastolic blood pressure as measured by 24-hour ABPM
Change in the composite index of antihypertensive drugs to reach target blood pressure
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in the composite index of antihypertensive drugs to reach target blood pressure. Drug Composite Index = Weight (number of classes of antihypertensive drugs) × (sum of doses)

secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From baseline to 6 months, 1 year post-procedure

secondaryOutcomes.description: Based on universal definition of acute myocardial infarction
Based on medical records under outcome committee's judge

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Rehospitalization due to heart failure incidence
secondaryOutcomes.description: Based on medical records under outcome committee's judge

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in serum creatinine or dialysis
